target.name: Reverion
target.founder: Stephan Herrmann, Felix Fischer, Jeremias Weinrich, Maximilian Hauck, Luis Poblotzki

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $79M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Reverion is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $79M to date. The valuation analysis relies on a comparable universe of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers (KoBold Metals at $3.0B Series C, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B Series C, and Amogy at $700M Series C), supplemented by a broader set of nine private companies and one public comparable in the energy transition space.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, anchoring on total capital raised as a proxy, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $150M. Peer-median analysis uses the median valuation across the three closest comparables, which stands at $1.0B, with observed range of $700M to $3.0B. The peer-median approach carries greater weight given the targeted filtering by sector and stage proximity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $128M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median estimate of $575M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate scaling rates and the heterogeneity of comparable technologies and capital efficiency within the climate and energy sector. Key sensitivities include technology maturity relative to peers, capital efficiency metrics, and the degree to which Series C multiples apply at Series B entry.
